name: Arroyomolinos de León,  Cerro Bonales, Cañaveral de León –  ruta circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around Arroyomolinos De León offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, significant peaks like Cerro Bonales and Tentudía Summit, and varied terrain suitable for off-road cycling. The region features a network of trails that traverse both natural areas and routes connecting to historical sites. Mountain bikers can expect a mix of ascents and descents through a landscape that includes forested sections and open vistas. Best mountain bike trails around Arroyomolinos De León The most popular mountain bike trail is Arroyomolinos de León, Cerro Bonales, Cañaveral de León – ruta circular, a difficult 30.0 miles (48.3 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 34 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation changes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra de Tentudía Monastery Loop, a difficult 39.2 miles (63.1 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and descents around the Sierra de Tentudía. Local mountain bikers also love the Monastery of Tentudía – Tentudía Summit loop from Cala, a 36.2 miles (58.3 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 51 minutes. Mountain biking around Arroyomolinos De León is defined by hilly terrain, historical landmarks, and challenging ascents. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to difficult mountain bike trails. The routes in Arroyomolinos De León are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 250 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Arroyomolinos De León's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Arroyomolinos De León?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails in the Arroyomolinos De León area, offering a diverse range of options for off-road cycling enthusiasts.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Arroyomolinos De León?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there is 1 easy mountain bike trail available. These trails are generally less demanding in terms of elevation and technical skill, making them suitable for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What kind of challenges can advanced mountain bikers expect in Arroyomolinos De León?
A: Advanced riders will find 20 difficult trails around Arroyomolinos De León. These routes feature significant elevation changes and cover long distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032487694/sierra-de-tentudia-monastery-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Tentudía Monastery Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 39.2 miles (63.1 km) with over 1,700 meters of ascent. Expect demanding climbs and technical descents.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike routes in Arroyomolinos De León?
A: While most trails in the area are rated moderate to difficult, the 1 easy trail can be a good starting point for families with some biking experience. It's always recommended to check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that form a loop in Arroyomolinos De León?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in the region are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995495135/arroyomolinos-de-leon-cerro-bonales-canaveral-de-leon-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyomolinos de León, Cerro Bonales, Cañaveral de León – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Tentudía – Tentudía Summit loop from Cala&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some scenic mountain bike trails in Arroyomolinos De León?
A: The region is known for its rolling hills and forested sections, offering scenic rides. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31457629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonales Peak – La Pantaneta Pond loop from Cabeza la Vaca&lt;/a&gt; provide expansive views and traverse diverse landscapes, including significant peaks and natural areas.

Q: What interesting landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails pass by historical and natural landmarks. You can encounter significant peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4048532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonales Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3164075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tentudía Summit&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4093491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Tentudía&lt;/a&gt; is another notable historical site accessible via some routes.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take to complete in this region?
A: The duration varies significantly based on the trail's length and difficulty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995495135/arroyomolinos-de-leon-cerro-bonales-canaveral-de-leon-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyomolinos de León, Cerro Bonales, Cañaveral de León – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 4 hours and 34 minutes, while longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38223402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Santa Marina – Historic Center of Aracena loop from Cala&lt;/a&gt; can take over 7 hours.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Arroyomolinos De León?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 7 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscape, challenging ascents, and the opportunity to explore both natural areas and historical sites.

Q: What is the terrain like for mountain biking around Arroyomolinos De León?
A: The terrain is characterized by rolling hills and significant peaks, including Cerro Bonales and Tentudía Summit. You'll encounter a mix of forested sections and open vistas, with routes traversing both natural landscapes and paths connecting to historical sites. Expect varied surfaces suitable for off-road cycling.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several spots with great views. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5345651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de la Cruz&lt;/a&gt; is a known viewpoint, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5154655" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Watchtower of Alto de Aguafría&lt;/a&gt; also provides panoramic vistas, often incorporated into longer routes.
tips: Arroyomolinos de León is certified as a Starlight Reserve Tourist Destination, thanks to its low light pollution. In addition, it has the only paragliding takeoff runway in the province of Huelva.

Mount Bonales (also known as Cerro Bonales or Cumbre Bonales) is, at 1,055 meters, the highest elevation in the Spanish province of Huelva. A fantastic tour of the natural park, enjoying cork oaks, holm oaks, oaks, meadows, and enjoying its flora and fauna.

The Cañaveral de León lagoon is a pool that collects groundwater from the Fuente Redonda spring and serves to irrigate the crops in the area. For a few years it has also been conditioned to be the municipal swimming pool of this town. In summer a treatment plant is started up to offer a pleasant bath on the hottest days.

waypoints.name: Bonales Summit (1,055 m) – Highest Point of Huelva
